evals.prompt: I need to redact PII from customer support messages before logging them. Here is an example message: 'Hi, I'm Alex Turner and I need help with my order. Please call me at 212-555-0147 or email me at alex.turner@example.com.' Build me a DSPy module that strips names, phone numbers, and email addresses.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy module using a two-pass approach: regex patterns for structured PII (email, phone) followed by a dspy.Predict call for contextual PII (names). The module's forward() method should return a redacted string with placeholders like [PERSON_NAME_1], [PHONE_1], [EMAIL]. Uses a seen-map dict to ensure consistent placeholders for repeated entities.

assertions.name: two_pass_pipeline
assertions.description: Implements regex pass first, then LM pass — not LM-only

assertions.name: regex_before_lm
assertions.description: Sends pre-masked text (not raw PII) to the LM call

assertions.name: uses_dspy_predict
assertions.description: Wraps a dspy.Signature with dspy.Predict for name detection

assertions.name: uses_seen_map
assertions.description: Uses a deduplication dict so the same entity always maps to the same placeholder

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM config uses dspy.LM with a generic provider and alternative comment

evals.prompt: I need to de-identify clinical notes for HIPAA Safe Harbor compliance. The notes contain patient names, DOBs, MRNs, provider names, phone numbers, and email addresses. Example: 'Patient Maria Flores, DOB: 07/22/1978, MRN: 4491203, was seen by Dr. Kevin Holt on March 10, 2025. Contact: mflores@gmail.com, (505) 555-3389.' Build a pipeline that removes all 18 PHI categories.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy module with HIPAA-specific regex patterns (MRN, NPI, DOB, dates, phone, email, SSN, URL, account numbers) plus a dspy.Predict call for contextual PHI (patient and provider names). Should handle at least the common HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers and return a de-identified string where all PHI is replaced with category placeholders.

assertions.name: hipaa_regex_patterns
assertions.description: Includes regex for MRN, DOB, phone, email, and date patterns beyond the basic set

assertions.name: lm_for_names
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Predict to detect patient and provider names that regex cannot catch

assertions.name: pre_masks_before_lm
assertions.description: Structured PHI is regex-masked before the text is sent to the LM

assertions.name: category_placeholders
assertions.description: Replacements clearly indicate the PHI category removed (e.g. [PATIENT_NAME], [MRN], [DOB])

assertions.name: no_assert_suggest
assertions.description: Does not use removed dspy.Assert or dspy.Suggest APIs

evals.prompt: I have a document where the same person is mentioned several times: 'Sarah Kim called at 9am. Sarah Kim said her account was locked. Please follow up with Sarah Kim by EOD.' The redacted output keeps giving each mention a different placeholder. How do I make the same entity always get the same replacement token?
evals.expected_output: An explanation and code fix showing that a seen-map dict (value to replacement) must be built before applying replacements. The same entity value should map to the same placeholder across all occurrences. The fix uses sorted replacement by descending length to prevent partial-match corruption, then applies all replacements from the seen dict in a single final pass.

assertions.name: seen_map_deduplication
assertions.description: Uses a dict mapping each entity value to its placeholder to ensure consistency

assertions.name: single_final_pass
assertions.description: Applies all replacements in one final pass on the original text, not incrementally

assertions.name: length_sorted_replacements
assertions.description: Sorts replacements by descending length to avoid partial-match corruption

assertions.name: identifies_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that the bug is missing deduplication, not an LM accuracy issue
